title: Niacinamide toning serum
subtitle: A water-based serum to address uneven skin tone and enlarged pores
excerpt: A lightweight water-based serum containing 5% niacinamide, vegetable glycerine, and aloe vera gel. Niacinamide (vitamin B3) is one of the most clinically supported cosmetic actives for reducing pore appearance and improving skin tone. Requires a preservative.
sourceNotes: Niacinamide efficacy at 5% for pore appearance and skin tone: Bissett and others, Niacinamide: A B vitamin that improves aging facial skin appearance (Dermatologic Surgery, 2005). Niacinamide-vitamin C interaction (temporary yellowing): documented; best separated by morning/evening routine. Preservative requirement for water-phase cosmetics: EU Cosmetics Regulation (EC) No 1223/2009.

recipe.yieldDescription: 1 small bottle (~30 ml, approximately 60 daily applications)
recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Keep niacinamide at exactly 5% of total formula weight for skin-active effect.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Ready to use immediately. Make in small batches (30 ml) for freshness.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Keep in the dropper bottle in a cool place. With a broad-spectrum preservative at the standard rate, shelf life is 3 months. Without a preservative, use within 2 weeks and keep in the fridge. Discard if cloudy, discoloured, or if an off smell develops.

glossaryTerms.definition: A preservative effective against both bacteria and mould. Required in all water-containing cosmetics for any practical shelf life. Common home-use options: Optiphen (0.5 to 1%), Geogard ECT, Liquid Germall Plus.

Apply a few drops to the inside of the forearm, leave 24 to 48 hours. Niacinamide at 5% is generally well-tolerated; a tingling or flushing sensation on first use usually settles after a few days of consistent use.

content.text: Niacinamide (vitamin B3) has strong clinical backing at 5%. It reduces visible pore size, improves uneven skin tone, and can lower sebum production in oily skin. It is also gentler than most active ingredients: no photosensitivity risk, no buffering needed. A temporary redness and tingling (niacin flush) on first use usually settles within a week of regular application. This serum is water-based, so it requires a

; without one, the formula keeps only 2 weeks in the fridge. The vegetable glycerine and aloe vera gel contribute a light humectant quality without adding oils or weight to the serum. Formula is 30 ml.

items.prepNote: 73% of formula; distilled, not filtered or tap
5% of formula by weight; weigh precisely
2% of formula; Optiphen or broad-spectrum equivalent; check your supplier's recommended usage rate

content.text: Weigh the niacinamide powder into a small bowl. Add 5 ml of the distilled water and stir until the niacinamide has fully dissolved; it dissolves readily at room temperature. Combine the remaining distilled water, vegetable glycerine, and aloe vera gel in the dropper bottle. Add the dissolved niacinamide solution. Add the preservative and fit the dropper top. Roll gently to mix. Label with the date. The serum is ready to use immediately.
on the inside of the forearm before applying to the face. A niacin flush (brief redness or tingling) on first application is common and usually settles with regular use.

Apply 2 to 4 drops to clean skin morning and evening before moisturiser. Press gently into the face and neck. Do not use at the same time as a vitamin C serum; the two can interact and produce a temporary yellow discolouration of the skin. Use vitamin C in the morning and niacinamide in the evening, or space them with a 30-minute gap.
Keep in a cool place away from light. With preservative at the recommended rate, the serum keeps 3 months. Discard if the serum turns yellow, becomes cloudy, or develops an off smell.
